---
title: "1.12 Objects: Instances of Classes"
subtitle: "Part 3: Creating Multiple Turtle Objects"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit1-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Creating Turtle Objects

### Many objects of one class type

When you write a class like the `Turtle` class, you can create many objects of that class type.

In the code below, two turtle objects are created: `yertle` and `myrtle`.

You can name your turtle and add a line in the `main` method to make it move.

## Object Creation Pattern

### Declare and create a new object

To create or declare a new object, write:

```java
// ClassName variableName = new ClassName(arguments);
Turtle yourTurtleName = new Turtle(habitat);
yourTurtlename.forward();
```

The class name is `Turtle`, and the variable name is the name you choose for a particular object.

## Coding Exercise

### `activecode:: TurtleTest3`

Can you add another turtle object to the code below?

The current code creates:

::: {.tight-list}
- a `World` object called `habitat`
- a `Turtle` object called `yertle`
- a `Turtle` object called `myrtle`
:::

Add a third turtle object created from the same `Turtle` class.

## Starter Code

### `TurtleTest3`

::: {.code-scroll}
```java
import java.awt.*;
import java.util.*;

public class TurtleTest3
{
    public static void main(String[] args)
    {
        World habitat = new World(300, 300);
        Turtle yertle = new Turtle(habitat);
        Turtle myrtle = new Turtle(habitat);
        // TODO: Add another Turtle object.

        yertle.forward();
        yertle.turnLeft();
        yertle.forward();

        myrtle.turnRight();
        myrtle.forward();

        habitat.show(true);
    }
}
```
:::

## Test Requirement

### Preserved Runestone check

Runestone checks the code for:

```java
new Turtle(habitat)
```

## Test Requirement

### Expected count

The test expects at least:

```text
3+ Turtles
```

That means the code should contain `new Turtle(habitat)` at least three times.

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should include

::: {.tight-list}
- use the pattern `ClassName variableName = new ClassName(arguments)`
- create a new `Turtle` object with a unique variable name
- pass `habitat` to the `Turtle` constructor
- leave the existing `yertle` and `myrtle` objects intact
- understand that all three objects are instances of the `Turtle` class
:::

## End

### 1.12 Part 3 complete

Part 4 continues with inheritance vocabulary and drawing a 7.
